ACTUAL RATING: MIXED
Steam forces a binary choice, so I have to vote 'Not Recommended'. The first 4 hours are a survival horror masterpiece. The last 4 hours are a linear, mechanically bankrupt shooter. I cannot recommend a game that completely abandons its own genre halfway through. If you buy this, get it on a massive sale and ONLY play on Madhouse difficulty.

If you are buying this strictly for a pure Survival Horror experience, you are going to be disappointed by the time the credits roll. Resident Evil 7 is a game with a massive identity crisis: it builds a flawless, terrifying foundation and then completely abandons it because Capcom was too terrified to commit to the tone.

THE GOOD:

    The Prologue: Exploring the Guest House in the daytime with zero context is arguably the best opening to any Resident Evil game ever. It lets the atmosphere and the silence do the talking.

    The Bakers: The first 40% of this game is a masterclass in game design. Jack Baker restricts your movement perfectly, and the psychological horror of the Marguerite section is the absolute peak of the game.

    The VHS Tapes: An incredible way to teach the player mechanics without breaking immersion. The "Happy Birthday" tape is literal genius and one of the best puzzles Capcom has ever designed, actively punishing you for spamming buttons.

THE BAD:

    The Mid-Game Cliff: The exact second you defeat Marguerite, the game stops being survival horror. It completely flatlines and turns into a highly unoriginal, linear corridor shooter. (Lucas Baker is still an amazing antagonist though)

    Unearned Power: The survival economy completely breaks. Your character is magically handed a submachine gun and a mountain of remote bombs that you didn't even have to work for, trivializing the rest of the game.

    The Final Act: The Salt Mines section is a painfully linear, incredibly short hallway sequence where the game morphs into the action-packed stupidness of RE4.

THE UGLY (Mechanics):

    Zero Enemy Variety: You fight exactly three types of Molded (Regular, Bladed, and a four-legged Licker-type). That's it. There are no grunts or unique variants to justify the arsenal you get.

    Brain-Dead AI: The Molded are slow, tethered to doorways (they despawn if you just walk into another room), and can be entirely cheesed just by holding the block button.

    Resource Overload: Even on Normal difficulty, you are handed so much Chem Fluid, enhanced handgun ammo, and shotgun shells that the tension completely evaporates.

THE EXPANSIONS (Not a Hero & End of Zoe):

    The Good: End of Zoe is pure, satisfying catharsis. Having a stalker enemy back is great, and Joe Baker's brawler mechanics—especially once you get the advanced gauntlet—are incredibly fun and exactly the kind of tactical combat Ethan lacked. Not a Hero introduces the "White Molded" (Regenerators) that require specific RAMROD ammo to kill; a brilliant survival-horror restriction that desperately belonged in the main campaign's Baker Estate.

    The Bad: Both DLCs are painfully short (about an hour each). Not a Hero ends with a boring final boss and feels like it either needed more development time or should have been scrapped entirely to flesh out Joe Baker's crafting system and extend End of Zoe.

THE VERDICT:
The environment design of the first half is incredible, but the combat balancing in the second half ruins it. They built a perfect haunted house and then handed you an assault loadout at the front door. The DLCs introduce great mechanical concepts, but suffer from incredibly short runtimes.

PSA: If you actually want to experience the Survival Horror loop Capcom originally intended, DO NOT play on Normal. Play on Madhouse Difficulty. It forces manual saves via Cassette Tapes, remixes item locations, makes Jack more aggressive, and is the only way the game actually justifies its own mechanics.
Very fun and scary game. This game has massive amounts of lore, jumpscares, and puzzles. The boss fights are also very fun. One of the best horror games out there.

RE7 takes the franchise into a new level of horror. The previous games that lean into survival horror, such as RE1 and RE2, are definitely great in their own ways - but it's not the type of horror that will actually scare you. RE7 was different, it is MUCH scarier than the other survival horror games in the franchise, even Outlast for me personally. I liked how they didn't overdo the jump scares, and instead focused on the atmosphere that made you nervous when walking down the hallways or solving puzzles. There was even this moment in the game where I was focused on solving the puzzles and when I turned, I just saw this kid's feet and I just screamed asf - no SFX or suspense music, just THAT.

In terms of the story, it also has an interesting one. Basically, you, as Ethan, looks for your missing wife in a residence and encounter a new BOW that can do terrifying things to others (not spoiling anything). I found this new form of BOW to be creative, as it is entirely different from what we are used to in the franchise - zombies. If you take the time to read the notes, you'll discover why the BOW behaves in that certain way and it actually makes sense how can this BOW be useful in warfare. The DLCs, Not A Hero and End of Zoe, also add important stuff to the closure of the story, and are there for some sick action (Joe Baker's cool asf). 

Overall, RE7 is such a great game. It just overtook RE4R as my favorite game in the franchise so far. Also, just wanted to mention, the theme song slaps man.

A game that I wanted to play since Senior High School. I love the way that despite using the first person camera, RE 7 still has the vibe of old Resident Evil where player has limited resource to fight unpredictable enemies' encounter. Also, the fight with Jack Baker was epic. Such a great game

Whenever I'm asked what my favorite game of all time is, I have two responses: Persona 3 Reload and Resident Evil 7.

It's a weird duo, but I consider this game as the perfect video game. It's a game everyone should play regardless if you're big into horror or not. 
Objectively speaking, this is not the best game of all time and i get that but it's mine for a different reason. I speak with a level of bias coming from this being my first horror game I played when I was quite young. It's important to say that, but I don't believe my bias comes from favoring from happier times but rather this being so good I am always biased in favor of it (if that makes sense).
The game is perfect in the sense where all of its flaws are not meaningfully impactful, everything in this game (including the DLCs, which I'll touch on later) serves and functional purpose. Nothing in this game was put there without meaning which is to say that nothing in this game can be called filler or bloat in my mind.

The main campaign is a tight experience, but also one you can explore for hours on end if you don't know what you're doing. There is a lot of secrets and a decent amount of items to get, but the most important being that there is immense replay value for rewards given during Madhouse difficulty or speed-running the game under 4 hours. I believe those incentives helped push this game into my perception of it being perfect. Even on the first experience, everything serves a purpose: whether that's the Baker Family running you the gauntlet, the atmosphere, the guns you pick up, the side puzzles you can do for extra rewards, and even the decision to replay is all done to keep you playing or serve as a nice stopping point.

The game has a decent amount of storytelling depth to it as well beyond just environmental and atmospheric depth. It's mainly helped by the DLCs in this regard as they provide more backstory on the Baker Family which is the point I'll provide reviews of the DLC.
---
End of Zoe: Serving as a proper finale of sorts to the game, it does a great job of reinforcing the theme of family. The beginning scenes and the ending scenes enforce that the most, but past that it's a blast that doesn't stay too long or too short, it also comes jam-packed with replay value and challenges to complete. Fist-fighting molded and Swamp Jack will never not be a good time.
---
Daughters: The weakest in the gameplay aspect, but also the strongest in showing who The Baker Family actually used to be before Eveline. It provides a massive amount of characterization that is noticeable if you compare it to a scene towards the tail end of the main game where Jack speaks to Ethan Winters as someone not driven mad. It's disturbing to see how quickly it happened, even if it did feel a tad rushed in pacing and it humanizes the characters properly to show you that it IS disturbing.
---
Nightmare: A fun mode, Clancy is an interesting case because he is an extremely small character in the main game. This mode serves as the games equivalent to The Mercenaries besides Jack's 55th Birthday, and despite the small amount of stuff in this DLC, it provides a fun experience with a lot of replay value in the form of rewards for more weapons or scrap.
---
Not A Hero: This is meant for more mainline fans of Resident Evil as it throws you in the boots of Chris Redfield. Like End of Zoe, it's a blast to play through since you can punch molded while also touching on an arc Chris' character had in RE6 as an exceptional man who gets his men killed. My main criticism is that it didn't do that theme well enough, but it's not enough of a large criticism where I'd deduct points from the overall DLCs.
---
Bedroom: My least favorite but also the most tense out of the DLC lineup. It's a small DLC in length (around 20-30 minutes or so) and doesn't do much for the game besides showing how crafty Clancy is and what was established in the main game of the Bakers being driven to insanity. It's not at all bad or even average, it's genuinely scary and even realistic in dialogue when Marguerite comes in the room to check on you and how she can shift from calm to anger in an instant.
---
21: This mode is arguably the most fun DLC besides End of Zoe, it's actually such a good and unique take of blackjack that I want it to become an actual multiplayer game with both its atmosphere and gimmick of trump cards. Again, it doesn't do much functionally to the story besides humanizing the Baker's victims (particularly Hoffman) in a cruel game of 21, but it hardly matters when the DLC is so fun. Whether that's reading the opponents moves or shifting a loss into a win, every choice in this DLC is something that matters. It's cruelty, at the risk of sounding morbid, is also something a lot of people should see given its inspirations from SAW.

----
These 2 DLCs are the only non-canon ones but are still worth discussing, these aren't needed to play but serve as the game's proper minigames:

Jack's 55th Birthday: It's a fun, yet disappointingly short mode. It's a mode that I believe is creative and has a fun gimmick, but that gimmick quickly gets old past the first 2 stages. It's not bad, its pretty good and comes with more replay value and builds for Mia depending on your time score within the game. The best part has to be the atmosphere, mini-bosses, and the ridiculous humor of the mode.
---
Ethan Must Die: My only complaint is that this mode didn't go on longer. It's a very cruel mode, serving as a roguelike mode for the game which is done incredibly well. There's explosive bosses, one-hit enemies, traps that can kill you in one go, lock-on turrets, and randomized drops for each run; however, despite how hard it can be, it's not so hard you give up and say "I can't beat it!". Luck is the main decider, but it also serves as a great test for tactics built by those who beat Madhouse difficulty in the main game and those who beat Joe Must Die. It's very fun, I highly recommend it!
--------
To end this review off, I'd like to impress upon you that this game is a **must play**. It's a game I believe any modern gamer should play to get a true taste for survival horror and a great introduction to Resident Evil. I believe even if I wasn't biased, I'd still put this game as my favorite game of all time.

Astonishing game. This is a revival, an instant jump from the flood of re6. re7 is delivering a high-notch atmosphere, cool puzzles, interesting storyline and fantastic gameplay. I mean no offense to re6, but this... damn, is this even a comparison? Overall some people say that the boat section was the worst part of the game. Seriously? I liked it the most tbh. Yeah the cave and final boss was a lil dragged-out, but as a totality this game still rocks. Yeah the game has some cons, like for example new-gen chris didn't rly age well. The game isn't that scary overall, people are overexaggerating in my opinion. Overall I'd say 8/10.
amazing horror game and great resident evil game with scary atmosphere and gross environment that make the name biohazard suitable.

Comparing this game to the previous resident evils, I will say it is a lot scarier. I feel as though the previous games focused more on the action aspect whereas this game definitely emphasized the horror. Overall, there isn't as much lore in this game compared to the others but regardless it was rlly fun to play! Great storytelling and graphics. ALSO I would say this game has the best RE song, its ALWAYS stuck in my head.

I’m super late to the party on this. I have been slowly working my way through all the Resident Evil games and catching up on stuff I had missed or neglected.

After what I consider the be a train wreck that was Resident Evil 6, this game has revived my enthusiasm for the series. 

It doesn’t have the third person camera the others do and instead opted for first person, which I think worked out really well. That, along with the natural bio horror style that resident evil is known for was an amazing combination.

My play through, without the DLC was about 8.5 hours. Definitely worth the price tag, and if you can get it on sale then even better.

Here is a video of part 1 of my play through, if you wanted to check it out:
https://youtu.be/S-3NhQftzTE

If you have a moment to subscribe (it’s free), that is greatly appreciated. Thank you.

Pretty good game as you might have got used to from the RE franchise, but this one was a little too short for me. Just when you get used to a sequence/main villain and start to enjoy going against them, the game moves on and kills them. In total it also felt very small-scale and the game didn't make up for it in scariness. Buy it on sale if you want to play.

Capcom's run of Resident Evil games after the RE6 debacle is incredibly good, and this one was the return to form. It's not as good as RE2 Remake, RE4 Remake, or Requiem, but it's better than RE3 Remake, on par with Village, and is probably the scariest RE game. Its story is preposterous, built on a shoddy foundation of wornout tropes and middling VA, but also lacking the camp that makes RE4 OG such a triumph of storytelling. Basically no enemy variety is its Achilles' Heel. 8.5/10.
Scary and well-made, but definitely hard in some places. It's back to the tried and true, but with a twist. I like it! Definitely recommend playing this one.

Resident Evil 7 = 8.5/10

Man, I have a special affection for this game, but in this review I’ll try to stay neutral. I spent basically the entire Xbox 360 and PS3 generation without gaming. I was a teenager, wanted to go out more, and ended up spending years away from video games. But when I saw the trailer for Resident Evil 7, I completely lost my mind. Back then, Mia looked insanely realistic — her hair, facial features — and the similarities to Outlast immediately caught my attention.

I genuinely became obsessed with the game and wanted to play RE7 no matter what, so I ended up buying a PS4. (At the time I already had a PC, but I still preferred playing on consoles.)

The experience was incredible. I absolutely loved this game. I thought the gameplay was amazing, and honestly, I never found it as terrifying as many people claimed. To me, this game feels a lot like those 1980s horror movies filled with cheesy gore and cheap practical effects.

Man, fighting Jack is almost hilarious at times, especially during the garage fight and when he grabs that giant pair of pruning shears.

Negative points:

- This game honestly could have had a different title and worked as a parallel story within the Resident Evil universe. Even though I really love this game, I still don’t truly feel like I’m playing Resident Evil. And honestly, making it a numbered Resident Evil game makes it even weirder hahaha.

- The ship section with Mia is extremely boring and should have been skippable. In RE9 I hate playing as Chloe, and in RE2 Remake I hate playing as Sherry, but at least those sections are short. With Mia on the ship, it feels like it never ends. It’s extremely irritating.

- There’s no option to skip cutscenes, and that makes replaying the game much more annoying.

After upgrading to a laptop capable of light gaming, I decided to re-purchase Resident Evil 7 since there is an ongoing sale, I've beaten this game close to a dozen times and even acquired the Platinum Trophy on PS4. I highly recommend this entry.

Resident Evil 7 – 10/10

Absolutely incredible. One of the best games I have ever played. I usually hate horror games because they freak me out, so this was a completely different experience for me. I loved everything about it. The game has a nasty, grimy atmosphere that makes you feel uncomfortable in the best way possible, and the scares got me at every turn.

As a huge fan of The Texas Chain Saw Massacre, this felt like a deep dive into that style of twisted, backwoods horror. The Baker family is unforgettable, and the game keeps the tension high from beginning to end. Nearly perfect from start to finish.

10/10.

A good RE game and introduction to a new character, Ethan. He is a badass in his own right and while he may not be as trained or professional as Chris or Leon, he is tough as nails and will do anything it takes for his wife. Game itself has good graphics and a couple of good scares, and I like the puzzles even though they are actually pretty simple. Oh and also, multiple endings to the main game as well as all the DLC so lots of replay value here!
RE7 is one of the most iconic games to ever come out and changed the entire tone of the resident evil universe. it will forever be one of my fav games. I streamed my latest play through on tiktok!

"Resident Evil 7 biohazard" was one of the thriller and tensing game I have played, it has photorealism you may wander, stories that might hook you into exploring, curiosity, and surprises are always waiting for you, one of the capcom games the best game ever made on the resident evil franchise, at first it may be scary to you, molded, jack and marguette, and dark and small rooms to explore, but once you play it again or lot of times, it may be the go to horror games to play with then you are bored or if you have banned footage, you may challenge yourself. Overall I would give this game 9.5/10 for rich story, creepiness, and lore about this game that unsettles your feeling when playing this game, recommended if you are looking forward to buy and play horror games for the first time.

Solid 7 not the best resident evil game still solid enough. its at its best during the first half but id say falls a bit off during the back. the whole house section with jack is brilliant if the game kept up with that level of quality it would prob be a top 5 re game. sadly the whole section you play as mia is soooo boring, ive played this game twice and every time i get to that mia part i just want the game to end. definitely  not something you want as your 3rd act of a game. plus the end boss fight is ass. Ethan is fine as ur mc not nearly as bad as he is in village, still he doesnt have the charm or personality as leon. he kind of just is an empty shell. its kind of funny hes going through all this ♥♥♥♥  and barely has like any emotion. so yea bad mc and mid ending other than that the gameplay and atmosphere from the main house all the way to the ship is fantastic and is the reason i continuted to play it both times i did.

Nice story, love the introduction of new characters, gives franchise a refreshment and saved it tbh.
9/10

Resident Evil 7 is one of the scariest games I have ever played.

The atmosphere is incredible. From the moment you step into the Baker estate, everything feels disturbingly believable. The environment, the sound design, the pacing, and the overall horror direction come together to create a constant feeling of dread. I was genuinely terrified for large parts of the game.

What impressed me most is how immersive it felt. Many horror games eventually stop being scary and become action games with horror elements. Resident Evil 7 never fully lost that tension for me. There were many moments where my main feeling wasn't "that was fun" but rather "I actually survived that." I mean that as a compliment. The game does such a good job of making you feel vulnerable that every victory feels earned.

Because of that, I would give one warning: this game can be genuinely stressful. If you're looking for a more relaxed experience, this might not be for you. For me, however, that intensity is exactly what made it so memorable.

The story is engaging, the characters are memorable, and the game does an excellent job balancing exploration, puzzles, resource management, and horror. It feels like a perfect modern interpretation of what Resident Evil can be.

I would honestly call it a masterpiece of horror game design.

One thing that surprised me was how much I enjoyed the DLCs, especially Not a Hero and End of Zoe. After spending so much time feeling stressed and hunted throughout the main game, those DLCs felt incredibly satisfying and almost therapeutic. They let me release all the tension that had built up over the course of the story. That is purely a matter of personal preference though, and I don't think they are better than the main game. The main campaign remains the heart of the experience and is exceptional in its own right.

Resident Evil 7 is not just a great Resident Evil game. It is one of the best horror games I have ever played, and one that I will never forget.

RE7 is the scariest game in the series outside of RE1 Remake and RE3
It has a very strong Sam Raimi Evil Dead + Texas Chainsaw Massacre vibe. This game is a straight-up love letter to the very first Resident Evil.
The Baker family is absolute kino and in many aspects this is better than both RE4 and RE8. Ethan is extremely relatable, while Mia is extremely unlikable (which I think was done on purpose).
But there's one major problem: There's only two enemy varieties in the entire base game, which is super weird because the DLCs actually have more enemy variety than the main game.
Still an extremely solid horror experience.
8/10

The revivor of the resident evil franchise, this game is so good. It gave us our boy Ethan and an incredible story that leads up to village which is also a fantastic game. One of the all time greats for sure, at least in terms of the single horror game genre of games.

This is my honest review regarding Resident Evil 7, which is a great game. I really enjoyed the character designs, and the horror elements were balanced perfectly, for its still scary but not that too scary until I can continue playing (I am bit softie regarding horror games). I also think Mia is a very attractive character, and Capcom has done an excellent job designing its female characters in recent years. Their characters look realistic while still being visually appealing. This is one of the reasons I appreciate Capcom's approach, especially when compared to some other studios *COUGH* NAUGHTY DOGS *COUGH*, but overall 9/10 since nothing Is ever perfect but very recommended to others to try this game

The best balance between horror and action in one game;  great game.
This is a scary ass game, I think you should play it. It basically redefined what a resident evil game would look like for the next 10+ years. Its good.

After experiencing RE4 classic, RE4 remake, and RE9, I've really only experienced the high-action based games so far, and this game has really shown me the true colors of the Resident Evil franchise. Being entirely horror and method based, I was on the edge of my seat throughout the entire game. There was no parry system, no roundhouse kick, and no abundance of ammo to save me. I needed to carefully plan what I needed to do while being chased or jumpscared left and right. I was completely hooked on the story as well, everything flowed perfectly, up to the very end, where I was met with the feeling of emptiness and shock after playing such a flawless game, just like the rest of the Resident Evils I've played so far. Amazing work as always Capcom.
Was really scared at first but i managed to finish the game and i'm glad that i did.
I hate jacks 55th birthday, no ethan should not die, no you are not a hero, no it is not the end of zoe. 10/10 game
not gonna lie it was scary but i really enjoyed chris and joe's parts. it felt satisfying to punch those freaks.

Best horror game that I have played in my life!

The game even runs with my ♥♥♥♥ PC.
Top notch experience. One of the best games in its category. The atmosphere of this game is second to none. The more you play the more it keeps on giving.

For my first ever Resident Evil game (and frankly my first real horror game,) I thought that Biohazard was a masterpiece of a game, especially for it being made in 2017. The overall atmosphere of the game from the very start to around the final act of the game is extremely well, and you can really tell the amount of love and detail there was that was put into the game. However, I have 1 minor and 1 major qualm with the game. The first is the boss fights. There is a nice handful of boss fights within the game, however all of them are for 4 specific characters, that being Jack and Marguerite Baker, your wife Mia, and Eveline, the main villain of the game. While the fights for Jack do feel well done, I can not say the same for the rest. All of Marguerites fights are just dragged on and boring, Mia's fights (I didn't do the last one) are just heavily scripted, and Eveline's are just extremely tedious. But my major con for this game is basically just the final act. You would expect the game to end after giving either Zoe Baker or Mia the serum, but no, your gonna play for maybe another hour in a boring cargo ship and a mineshaft, before fighting a boring scripted boss fight. I sincerely don't know how I made it through the ship, but I'm glad I did. Is this better than 8? No. But does it execute horror better? Yes, 8.5/10
One of the best survival horror games. I know i am bit late to this game, but i am happy i played it.

A good game, really appreciate how Resident Evil 7 brought back the horror to the franchise. While probably not one of my favourite Resident Evil games it's nice to have a break from the action horror that RE4 started.

A phenominal and horrifying experience filled with twists and turns that I didn't expect, one of the best horror experiences I've had so far and a great beginner entry to the franchise, as this is the first game I've played

The DLC are also all phenominal in their own rights as well, the banned tapes focusing in on the different aspects of the main game, the Not a Hero DLC which brings some more action elements into the game to make it similar to previous entries, and End of Zoe where you play as Joe Baker and just go '♥♥♥♥ it, I'm just going to throw straight hands with these mold monsters' while also tying up the story nicely.

A solid 5/5, I'd recommend everyone to player this game if they have the stomach for it
Fun, scary and heartbreaking. Wish I could forget I ever played it just to experience it again for the first time.
One of my first, AND favorites in the franchise. I have played it over a few times now and cannot recommend it enough as a first-person horror game AND zombie game!!

One of the most intense Resident Evil games I've ever played. You will be hunted and stalked throughout bedrooms and hallways, fighting for your life against the Baker family and monsters made from mold. There are a lot of puzzles to solve and areas to explore in the dilapidated Louisiana estate this game takes place in. The Baker family themselves are some of the most unsettling antagonists I've ever seen, taking their time to torment and torture you. Their boss fights are unlike anything I've experienced, transforming the environment against you like you've been trapped in a spider's web. Playing through RE7 I constantly felt this desperate need to escape, and I loved it.

The game also features quite a bit of DLC, though only three are relevant to the story. 'Daughters' is a short and terrifying prequel showing the Baker family before their transformation. 'Not a Hero' is much more action oriented and follow Chris Redfield's hunt for Lucas. And finally 'End of Zoe', a rescue story where your main weapons are your two fists! Box the molded to death, throw spears, and snap necks. As ridiculous as the DLC may sound, it's actually a ton of fun and personally my favorite of RE7's DLC content.

If you're looking for a survival horror experience with a great story and heart pumping gameplay, I cannot recommend RE7 enough.
Es una pasada de juego realmente lo jugué sin verme ni un gameplay ni spoilers y fue una experiencia buenísima recomendadisimo
Resident Evil in first person is very fun and I like the combat system
played once on console and once on pc. game still fun af. recommend to any RE fan, new or old.

this game is one of my favourite resident evil games already, and i haven't even beat most of the other games yet.

the game genuinely scared me at times and the atmosphere really makes the game have a very unsettling theme, which i fw ALOT.

the gameplay is really good aswell, i had fun using most of the weapons however enemy variety does lack alot once you get near the endgame

the baker family are also pretty good, lucas and jack are characters i like alot.

overall? 8/10. i enjoyed playing as ethan walk em down winters
This is the greatest, most terrifying, most immersive experience I’ve ever had in a horror game. I highly recommend.

Great horror experience the first few times, becomes really cancerous to 100%

This game falls into the category where those last few tough achievements are either super stressful, demanding, or pure RNG, you'll likely have a good 100 hours of playtime, most of it grinding for the last handful of achievements
great game overall i would recommend to other players

My favourite Resident Evil game by far, the key game really getting me into horror in 2017. Played on Xbox then recently got this in the Capcom sale and 100% it on Steam feels nice.

RE7 isn't a perfect game, the main character Ethan is a muted (literally and figuratively), but for what it is, I really enjoy it.
Ethan being a more relatable protagonist is refreshing, his main goal of just wanting to meet and save his wife is understandable and easy to get behind. A seemingly normal man with a simple goal.

The gameplay is engaging, the atmosphere is eerie and unsettling in the Baker's estate, a big home turned into what it is now, this is my favourite area in the game.

The latter half of the game is weaker, which, learning more into the game's development, it makes sense, though it is undeniable a weaker game for it.

The DLCs for the game are numerous, the number of hours on 21 I've spent alone is crazy, it's just ends up being such a fun logic/puzzle game with a bit of randomisation.

If you like horror or you just want to play a good single player game, please give RE7 a shot.
great game,not ever replaying it again (♥♥♥♥ myself)
gotta be the best game in the entire series (besides the boat part ts was ass)

I was told it was scary, however it wasn't the case for me. If anything it was a jolt of panic, or impatience. To the unexperienced player, do not play this game until you play the ones before it. This game was good, I look forward to playing Village.

"Welcome to the family, son."

RE7 is a masterfully crafted nightmare. It’s rare to see a game where the environmental design, lighting, sound design, animations, enemy AI, and voice acting all pull equal weight to create such a thick, suffocating atmosphere.

It doesn't rely on cheap tricks—the whole package is just expertly put together to keep your heart rate through the roof. 10/10.

Fantastic Game it really is a return to form for resident evil great callbacks to previous games item Management and Good Combat Story is Good Voice Acting is great The Enemy Variety Just sucks as you only fight four Mold variants the normal the bladed the four legged and the fat one Boss Fights are a mixed bag for me with the best one in my opinion being the 2nd Bossfight with Jack where you fight Him with a chainsaw The Game is also Optimized Very Well as i was able to run it consistently with 60fps with my laptop although i had to lower some of the graphics but the game still looked great even in lower graphics Overall I Give this game a 8.9 out of 10
One of the best resident evil game by far, totally changes the way you look at RE series after the 6th installment
Completed in 7 hours, an interesting story and gameplay. For Fans of RE.
Upon arriving at the residence, it was clear that all inhabitants were extremely evil.  The house was untidy to the point where it would constitute a legitimate biohazard. 10/10
Absolute masterpiece start to finish it would be diabolical to have not beaten this game and see both endings at the bare minimum.

Really good game, with a lot of gameplay. It’s not too hard, and the bosses are interesting to say the least, with new mechanics and tactics every time. The spooky theme has its own charm, and the house exploration and design are amazing.

The last chapter/the ending is a bit disappointing. The gameplay is fine, but it really feels linear. Recommended if you’ve already played other Resident Evil 4 / Resident Evil 3 / Resident Evil 2 games, although this one feels very different.

The DLCs are fine but not worth the price, so either wait for a sale or just don’t buy them. The gameplay can feel really unfair and frustrating, not for everyone (like “21,” which is based mostly on pure luck with a bit of skill, so meh).

Total rating: around 9.5/10 for the main game and 6.5/10 for the DLCs.

THIS GAME IS SCARY.
This game scared me so bad, I sharted so loud that the neighbors thought I shot myself with a revolver.

Very good game. Not quite as good as RE4 but it's close. Best stalker enemies in any RE game, Jack Baker is awesome. Only part I didn't really like was [spoiler]having to choose between giving the antidote to Zoe or Mia. Mia didn't have enough character development for it to make sense to choose her, despite choosing her giving the better ending. [/spoiler]

WOWIEE! Great game. Residents are evil for sure, but the residence takes precedent. Characters are great & the low story (mold) is easily the coolest in the franchise. Played both 7 & 8 before writing reviews, definitely like 8 more but this game was an absolute shock & a great change of pace. Looks terrible but then I realized that all the remakes came out after on the new engine so i’ll give it a pass. Eveline might be the evilest resident of all time, pure evil as apposed to evil for aura’s sake. DLC is really cool, I like blackjack but was a bit disappointed there was no CBT involved in the blackjack mini game. Something for everyone, gamblers, masochists & moldf lovers (milf + mold). Felt a little stuck between old & new games, they didn’t really have it all figured out but nobody does in this great big world. Something to think about? Black cops. + rate for diversity, - rate for him getting shoveled. Kinda upset they made the punch the ♥♥♥♥ gamemode not chris, but I like Joe well enough. Chris looks dumb & weak. Chris looks like a redditor. Chris is not sexy anymore in this game, capcom FIX IT !!!!

[h1]A true savior of the series [/h1]

I enjoyed my experience with this game. In terms of gameplay and atmosphere, it's very well made. As for the puzzles, I'd say the difficulty is normal, and the puzzles are meaningful and serve the story. The characters are well-developed, including Clancy's personality through the video tapes we find. And finally, let me add that this game saved the Resident Evil series.

And for achievement hunters, I should say that perfecting the main game is a pleasure, especially the experience of playing on MAD HOUSE difficulty, but some achievements in the DLCs can be time-consuming and annoying.
tolong jangan beli game ni , game ini sangat menakutkan !!

Just accomplished madhouse difficulty, time to review. This is my first Resident Evil game in which I have played through and I most certainly enjoyed my time. Looking to 100% the achievements, that's how much fun I have had playing. What a captivating storyline, loved the horror/eeriness, and Ethan is such an awesome character. Luckily i picked this up during the capcom sale which comes with RE8, so i will be looking forward to continuing Ethan's storyline. I recommend this game for anyone who enjoys great character/story building, eerie settings, exploration in game. Have to reiterate, Ethan is such a cool character!!!!!

The 7th installment in the resident evil series (not sure if it's actually the 7th one) was the first one i ended up playing with no regrets so far.
After watching a bit of gameplay from the previous games i'd say this is the turning point of the franchise, dare i say the scariest one too. It's a mix of action, adventure and a hell of a lot of inventory management which i liked a lot personally and ended up making the game a tad bit more difficult. Gunplay feels fun, new areas constantly keep the game from becoming stale and puzzles aren't too difficult for your average player and bossfights were also very well done, kudos to capcom for being so consistent with their games! Overall i'd recommend this game for people who obviously like horror elements mixed with puzzles and a lot of bossfights :)
8/10
Ethan you durable mf

Banger, best horror game of its time by miles. Same replay value as most of the other entries, the only negative being that the cutscenes are in-game and unskippable for the most part. 

DLCs are also great, whether they be minigames or story additions, definitely worth it.

Always heard about how this game saved the re franchise so I was very excited to finally try this one out. It puts a lot more emphasis on the horror I think it did very well and gave some good scares throughout. The Baker family are terrifying antagonists especially Jack, although the rest of the enemies definitely lack variety imo. It's a great game 8/10

Great story, the Baker house is excellent, but enemy variety is lacking and the second major area isn’t super great. Not bad, but not great especially being compared to the Baker house at the beginning. Haven't played the Banned Footage DLCs, but Not A Hero + End of Zoe are nice additions to round off the Baker family story.

I'm not a fan of hillbilly horror, so this didn't strike me as much as many others in terms of enjoyment, but it only brings it down to about an 8/10 for me.

First time I've played through a horror game in a very long time and I loved it.

Ever since picking RE7 up again up until it was finished, I can describe this game in one particular way. It feels a lot like back when you were a kid in the middle of the night, having that feeling of being watched. The claustrophobic you get, while also being around a crazy family really plays hard into it.

Aside from the feeling I got, the horror itself is gorey, disgusting and quite tense. There's also some action involved, though the action can feel a bit stale at points and I don't think it's this game's strong suit. I am comfortable looking past that though as the immersive atmosphere completely outshines any weak points.

Overall, absolutely love this game. If you want to have an immersive, dark experience, GO FOR IT

I wouldn't say its my favorite RE game so far, but the intro is an absolute banger. Atmosphere and setting is perfect for a horror game. Visually holds up pretty well for a 9 year old game too. I think I appreciate most that this game kinda kicked off the RE revival so to speak.

Fantastic game. 100% recommend if you love horror. Definitely worth the price but keep in mind it goes on sale very frequently, so up to you.

If you are a fan of movies like Evil Dead, Texas Chainsaw Massacre, Saw, Halloween, etc, I think you'll appreciate this game a little more than most because of all of the homages to classic horror films.

Due to its first-person perspective, Resident Evil 7 is one of my least favourite Resident Evil games. However, I won't deny that it is an absolutely great game. I found the new enemy variety very interesting and welcome to the franchise, and the game's story is one of the best. The DLC is something else entirely. The developers clearly put a lot of effort into it, and it paid off. I highly recommend this game if you're looking for true survival horror
Best Re Game with best DLC. DLC completes the game and tells the stories of all involved.

20/20 game! I 100% recommend this to anyone, especially the Gold Edition. All of the DLC is fire—especially "21" if you're into card games. I absolutely love the base game, the story, and the End of Zoe DLC.
